file_name: EMERALDHEALTHTHERAPEUTICSINC_06_10_2020-EX-4.5-CONSULTING AGREEMENT - DR. GAETANO MORELLO N.D. INC..txt

perturbation.original_text: The Company may terminate this Agreement and the Contractor's engagement for Cause at any time on written notice to the Contractor. The date of termination will be the date specified in the written notice and may be, in the sole discretion of the Company, the same day the notice is given to the Contractor, or such later date as the Company may decide.
perturbation.changed_text: The Company may terminate this Agreement and the Contractor's engagement for Cause at any time on written notice to the Contractor. The date of termination will always be 30 days from the notice and may not be, in the sole discretion of the Company, the same day the notice is given to the Contractor, or such later date as the Company may decide.
perturbation.explanation: By modifying the clause in Section 5.3, we create a contradiction regarding the termination date for cause. The original text allows the Company to terminate 'at any time' and specifies the termination date 'may be' the same day as the notice. The modified text mandates a 30-day notice period, contradicting the immediate termination option and the freedom of the termination date from the original clause. This contradicts section 5.3 as the change mandates that the date of termination will always be 30 days from the notice, removing the flexibility of immediate termination and is thus is contradicting the freedom that was implied when the text said 'The date of termination will be the date specified in the written notice and may be, in the sole discretion of the Company, the same day the notice is given to the Contractor, or such later date as the Company may decide'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Emeralds' Right to Terminate this Agreement for Cause. The Company may terminate this Agreement and the Contractor's engagement for Cause at any time on written notice to the Contractor. The date of termination will always be 30 days from the notice and may not be, in the sole discretion of the Company, the same day the notice is given to the Contractor, or such later date as the Company may decide.
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 5.3 introduces a strong contradiction. The original text allowed the Company to terminate 'at any time' with the termination date at their discretion, possibly on the same day as the notice. The changed text mandates a 30-day notice period ('will always be 30 days from the notice'), directly contradicting the possibility of immediate termination and the Company's discretion over the termination date. This contradicts the original flexibility, creating a clear and enforceable conflict as defined by the 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: On the termination of the Contractor's engagement, the Contractor shall return to Emerald all property belonging to Emerald in the Contractor's possession or control. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Contractor will be entitled to keep and retain his or her laptop computer, office computer and smart phone.
perturbation.changed_text: On the termination of the Contractor's engagement, the Contractor shall return to Emerald all property belonging to Emerald in the Contractor's possession or control. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Contractor will be entitled to keep and retain his or her laptop computer, office computer and smart phone, depending on the reason for termination as solely determined by the company.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause in Section 5.6 clearly states that upon termination, the contractor must return all Emerald property, *except* for their laptop, office computer, and smartphone. However, the modified clause adds the condition that retaining these items is 'depending on the reason for termination as solely determined by the company.' This creates a contradiction. Originally, the contractor had a guaranteed right to keep those items regardless of the reason for termination. Now, that right is conditional and at the Company's discretion. This directly conflicts with the 'Notwithstanding the foregoing' clause, which was meant to create an exception to the general return policy, as in some cases the contractor may now be required to return such items depending on the reason of termination.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Return of Property. On the termination of the Contractor's engagement, the Contractor shall return to Emerald all property belonging to Emerald in the Contractor's possession or control. Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Contractor will be entitled to keep and retain his or her laptop computer, office computer and smart phone, depending on the reason for termination as solely determined by the company.
perturbation.justification: The original text states, "Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Contractor will be entitled to keep and retain his or her laptop computer, office computer and smart phone." The changed text modifies this to "Notwithstanding the foregoing, the Contractor will be entitled to keep and retain his or her laptop computer, office computer and smart phone, depending on the reason for termination as solely determined by the company." This introduces a condition on the contractor's entitlement to retain the specified items, which directly contradicts the unconditional entitlement initially granted by the 'Notwithstanding the foregoing' clause. The conditionality creates an inconsistency and uncertainty about the contractor's rights upon termination, as the contradicted text in the prompt demonstrates.
